prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "单腿跳" (single-leg hop) exercise, side view, facing right, freeze-frame photographic style. Person posture: Photographic freeze-frame showing an athlete suspended IN MID-AIR during a forward single-leg hop. EVIDENCE OF AIRBORNE POSITION: about 15-25 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below the right foot (the hopping foot) — the floor/ground is rendered with a small visible shadow far below the body. The LEFT LEG is HELD UP throughout the entire hop with the left knee bent at 90 degrees, left thigh horizontal — the LEFT FOOT NEVER TOUCHES THE GROUND in this drill. Only the RIGHT LEG provides propulsion; in this freeze-frame the right leg is mid-flight with knee slightly bent. Arms swing for momentum. Torso slightly forward-leaning showing forward motion. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: NOT a regular running stride with both feet alternating on the ground — both feet are CURRENTLY OFF THE GROUND, the body is suspended in the air, and the LEFT foot has been held up the entire time.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles and quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the hopping leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: hamstring group and core muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "小腿 / 股四头肌" pointing to the calf and quadriceps of the hopping leg; "臀大肌" pointing to the gluteus maximus; "腘绳肌 / 核心" pointing to the hamstring and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
